content.problemStatement:
You are given a positive integer MMM and an array A (1‚â§Ai‚â§M)A \ (1\leq A_i \leq M)A (1‚â§Ai‚Äã‚â§M) consisting of NNN positive integers.

Find the **lexicographically largest** array BBB such that:


‚à£B‚à£=N|B|=N‚à£B‚à£=N;
1‚â§Bi‚â§M1 \leq B_i \leq M1‚â§Bi‚Äã‚â§M for all 1‚â§i‚â§N1 \leq i \leq N1‚â§i‚â§N;
Ai=gcd‚Å°(B1,B2,‚Ä¶,Bi)A_i = \gcd(B_1, B_2, \ldots, B_i)Ai‚Äã=gcd(B1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Bi‚Äã), where gcd‚Å°\gcdgcd denotes the greatest common divisor.
Note that the input AAA guarantees that BBB always exists.

For two arrays XXX and YYY, both of size NNN, the array XXX is said to be lexicographically larger than array YYY, if, in the first position where XXX and YYY differ, Xi>YiX_i \gt Y_iXi‚Äã>Yi‚Äã.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space separated integers NNN and MMM ‚Äî the  length of array AAA and upper bound on array elements.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã representing the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the lexicographically largest array BBB, satisfying the given conditions.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 5‚ãÖ1045 \cdot 10^45‚ãÖ104.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The only possible array is B=[1]B = [1]B=[1] which satisfies the given conditions.
Test case 222: The only possible array is B=[2,1]B = [2, 1]B=[2,1] which satisfies the given conditions.
Here A1=B1=2A_1 = B_1 = 2A1‚Äã=B1‚Äã=2, and A2=gcd‚Å°(B1,B2)=1A_2 = \gcd(B_1, B_2) = 1A2‚Äã=gcd(B1‚Äã,B2‚Äã)=1.
Test case 444: The lexicographically largest valid array is B=[2,4,4,4]B = [2, 4, 4, 4]B=[2,4,4,4]. Some other arrays that are valid include [2,2,2,2],[2,2,4,2],[2,2,4,4][2, 2, 2, 2], [2, 2, 4, 2], [2, 2, 4, 4][2,2,2,2],[2,2,4,2],[2,2,4,4].
